  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはボードウォーゲームです。",
      "それは師団レベルの戦術ウォーゲームです。",
      "それはワルシャワ条約機構による西ヨーロッパ侵攻をシミュレートするゲームです。",
      "それは1970年代に西ヨーロッパを防衛するNATO軍に対するワルシャワ条約機構軍の奇襲攻撃をシミュレートするゲームです。",
      "それは多数のユニットを扱うゲームです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは中央ヨーロッパのヘックスグリッドマップを持っています。",
      "それはデンマークからスイスまでの南北軸を持っています。",
      "それはポーランドからベルギーまでの東西軸を持っています。",
      "それは1ヘックスあたり15マイルのスケールを持っています。",
      "それは36ページのルールブックを持っています。",
      "それは3ページのルール訂正を持っています。",
      "それはダイカットカウンターを持っています。",
      "それは4つのシナリオを持っています。",
      "それは戦術核兵器を持っています。",
      "それは政治的ルールを持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは1ヘックスあたり15マイルのスケールを特徴としています。",
      "それはワルシャワ条約機構軍による奇襲攻撃を特徴としています。",
      "それはNATO軍による西ヨーロッパ防衛を特徴としています。",
      "それは1970年代を舞台設定として特徴としています。",
      "それは一部のシナリオにおける戦術核兵器の搭載を特徴としています。",
      "それは敵ユニット破壊によって得られる勝利点を特徴としています。",
      "それは都市の占領または奪還によって得られる勝利点を特徴としています。",
      "それは敵対行為開始後20ターン続くシナリオを特徴としています。",
      "それは8つの異なるユニット移動オプションを特徴としています。",
      "それは補給線、戦術核兵器、戦闘における複雑さを特徴としています。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それはワルシャワ条約機構による西ヨーロッパ侵攻のシミュレーションに使用されます。",
      "それは1970年代に西ヨーロッパを防衛するNATO軍に対するワルシャワ条約機構軍の奇襲攻撃のシミュレーションに使用されます。",
      "それは師団レベルでの戦闘のシミュレーションに使用されます。",
      "それは両陣営が攻撃の準備をする間、侵攻を遅延させるために使用されます。",
      "それは両陣営が最高警戒態勢にあるシナリオのシミュレーションに使用されます。",
      "それは戦術核兵器を含むシナリオのシミュレーションに使用されます。",
      "それは勝利点に基づいて勝者を決定するために使用されます。",
      "それは他のウォーゲームとの複雑さの比較に使用されます。",
      "それは満足のいくゲーム体験を提供するために使用されます。",
      "それは政治的ルールを伴う軍事行動に使用されます。"
    ],

IsA: It is a tactical wargame at the divisional level.
It is a game that simulates an invasion of Western Europe by the Warsaw Pact.
It is a game that simulates a surprise attack by Warsaw Pact forces against NATO forces defending Western Europe in the 1970s.
It is a game that involves a large number of units.
HasA: It has a hex grid map of Central Europe.
It has a north-south axis of Denmark to Switzerland.
It has an east-west axis of Poland to Belgium.
It has a scale of 15 miles per hex.
It has a 36-page rulebook.
It has 3 pages of rules errata.
It has die-cut counters.
It has four scenarios.
It has tactical nuclear weapons.
It has political rules.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a scale of 15 miles per hex.
It is characterized by a surprise attack by Warsaw Pact forces.
It is characterized by the defense of Western Europe by NATO forces.
It is characterized by the 1970s as the setting.
It is characterized by the inclusion of tactical nuclear weapons in some scenarios.
It is characterized by victory points earned by destroying enemy units.
It is characterized by victory points earned by capturing or recapturing cities.
It is characterized by scenarios lasting 20 Turns once hostilities commence.
It is characterized by eight different options for unit movement.
It is characterized by complexity in supply lines, tactical nuclear weapons, and combat.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating an invasion of Western Europe by the Warsaw Pact.
It is used for simulating a surprise attack by Warsaw Pact forces against NATO forces defending Western Europe in the 1970s.
It is used for simulating combat at the divisional level.
It is used for delaying an invasion as both sides prepare for an attack.
It is used for simulating a scenario with both sides at peak readiness.
It is used for simulating a scenario with tactical nuclear weapons.
It is used for determining a winner based on Victory Points.
It is used for comparing complexity with other wargames.
It is used for providing a satisfying game experience.
It is used for military action with political rules.
PublishedIn: It was published in Strategy & Tactics #220.
It was published by Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
ProducedBy: It was produced by Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
It was produced by Decision Games.
It was produced by Compass Games.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a hex grid map of Central Europe.
It consists of a 36-page rulebook.
It consists of 3 pages of rules errata.
It consists of die-cut counters.
It consists of four scenarios.
It consists of special rules for Denmark.
It consists of eight different options for unit movement.
It consists of rules for supply lines.
It consists of rules for tactical nuclear weapons.
It consists of rules for combat.
KnownFor: It is known for simulating operational combat in Europe in the 1970s.
It is known for its back-and-forth nature.
It is known for its complexity.
It is known for being a classic game.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Warsaw Pact.
It is associated with NATO forces.
It is associated with Western Europe.
It is associated with Jim Dunnigan.
It is associated with Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
It is associated with Decision Games.
It is associated with Compass Games.
It is associated with Nicholas Palmer.
It is associated with Ralph Vickers.
It is associated with John D. Burtt.
About: It is about an invasion of Western Europe by the Warsaw Pact.
It is about a surprise attack by Warsaw Pact forces against NATO forces defending Western Europe in the 1970s.
It is about operational combat in Europe in the 1970s.
It is about the defense of Western Europe.
It is about the Warsaw Pact player delaying an invasion.
It is about both sides preparing for an attack.
It is about both sides being at peak readiness.
It is about the addition of tactical nuclear weapons.
It is about destroying enemy units.
It is about capturing or recapturing cities.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Comprehensive Guide to Board Wargaming.
It was influenced by a 1977 poll conducted by SPI.
It was influenced by the special rules for Denmark.
It was influenced by the rules for supply lines.
It was influenced by the rules for tactical nuclear weapons.
It was influenced by the rules for combat.
It was influenced by the rules for unit movement.
It was influenced by the fall of Saigon.
It was influenced by the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan.
